title: Mr. Chips
author: James Hilton
description: A timeless tale of a gentle, old schoolmaster whose life touches generations of students. A warm, nostalgic story about love, duty, loss and the passing of time through decades at an English boys' school.

title: Thomas Calculus
author: George B. Thomas Jr.
description: The definitive calculus textbook used worldwide in universities. Covers single and multivariable calculus with thorough explanations, worked examples, and exercises. Essential for engineering and science students.

title: Essentials of Computer Science
author: Paul D. Crutcher
description: Understand essential computer science concepts and skills. This book focuses on foundational concepts upon which expertise in specific areas can be developed, including computer architecture, programming language, algorithm and data structure, operating systems, computer networks, distributed systems, security, and more.

title: The Power of Now
author: Eckhart Tolle
description: A spiritual guide to living in the present moment and achieving enlightenment. Tolle explores the concept of mindfulness and how to transcend the ego to find inner peace and happiness.

title: Islamic Jurisprudence
author: Mohsin Arif
description: A comprehensive book on Islamic Jurisprudence written from an examination point of view. Students can use this book for preparing top-level assignments and also for securing top positions in exams. Covers all topics of Islamic Jurisprudence in a very simple and easy to understand language.

title: Fundamentals of Database Systems
author: Ramez Elmasri
description: A comprehensive guide to database systems, covering fundamental concepts, design, and implementation. Essential for students and professionals looking to deepen their understanding of database management, SQL, normalization, and transaction processing.

title: Business Economics
author: CA Jasmeet Singh
description: A thorough introduction to business economics covering micro and macro economic principles, market structures, national income, fiscal and monetary policy, and international trade. Ideal for commerce and MBA students.

title: Sharah Kulyat-e-Iqbal
author: Prof. Hameedullah Shah Hashmi
description: A detailed commentary and explanation of the complete poetic works of Allama Muhammad Iqbal. Covers Bang-e-Dra, Bal-e-Jibril, Zarb-e-Kaleem and the Persian works with Urdu translation and scholarly notes.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
author: Thomas H. Cormen
description: The comprehensive reference for algorithms commonly known as CLRS. Covers a broad range of algorithms in depth while making their design and analysis accessible. Each chapter is self-contained and can be used as a unit of study.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of human history from the Stone Age to the Silicon Age. Harari explores how Homo sapiens came to dominate the earth, examining the cognitive, agricultural, and scientific revolutions that shaped civilizations.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: One of the most beloved novels in the English language. A witty and romantic story of Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy navigating manners, morality, and marriage in Regency-era England.

title: Pakistan: A Modern History
author: Ian Talbot
description: A comprehensive historical account of Pakistan from its creation in 1947 to the present day. Examines the political, social, and economic forces that have shaped the nation's turbulent journey.
